metadata.description: Specifies the Azure region where the resources are deployed. Defaults to the current resource group's location.
Name of the Azure Web App. Must be 2–60 characters using lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens. Defaults to a generated value based on 'zappermft' and the current UTC timestamp.
SKU tier for the App Service Plan. Choose from Basic (B1–B3), Standard (S1–S3) or Premium v2/v3 (P1v2–P3v3).
Name of the Azure Container Registry (ACR), excluding the .azurecr.io suffix.
Name of the resource group that contains the ACR.
Subscription ID of the ACR. Defaults to the current subscription.
Indicates whether the container registry is public (true) or private (false). When true, anonymous pulls are allowed.
Name of the container image repository.
Tag of the container image.
Specifies whether to use an existing PostgreSQL database ('existing') or create a new one ('new').
Full connection string for an existing PostgreSQL database (e.g., postgres://user:password@host:5432/dbname). Required when 'databaseOption' is 'existing' and Key Vault integration is disabled.
Secret key used by the application for JWT encryption. Auto-generated GUID for security.
Name of the PostgreSQL server to create when 'databaseOption' is 'new'.
Name of the PostgreSQL database to create when 'databaseOption' is 'new'.
Administrator username for the new PostgreSQL server when 'databaseOption' is 'new'.
Administrator password for the new PostgreSQL server. Auto-generated GUID for security.
Indicates whether to assign the AcrPull role to the Web App so it can pull images from the ACR.
A unique timestamp used to force the Web App to restart on each deployment.
Email address of the initial admin user for the application. Must be a valid email format.
Name of the initial organization. Up to 24 characters; may include letters, digits, spaces, underscores or hyphens.
Determines whether to use an existing Azure Key Vault. If true, provide 'existingKeyVaultId' and optionally 'existingKvTenantId'; otherwise a new vault may be created.
Resource ID of an existing Key Vault to use for storing secrets.
Tenant ID associated with the existing Key Vault. Provide this only if the vault is in a different tenant.
Name for a new Key Vault. Must be 3–24 characters using lowercase letters, numbers or hyphens. Required when not using an existing vault and Key Vault integration is enabled.
Name of the Key Vault secret that stores the database connection string.
Name of the Key Vault secret that stores the Azure Maps API key for geo-fencing.
Indicates whether the Web App reads the database URL from Key Vault at runtime. Set to false to pass the connection string via an environment variable.
Specifies whether to create a new Key Vault ('new') or use an existing one ('existing').
Role assignment for access to blob storage used by the application. Allowed values are 'Reader', 'Contributor' and 'Owner'.
Specifies whether to use a separate Azure Container App for creating large ZIP downloads.
Threshold size in megabytes at which zipping is offloaded to the Container App. Must be a whole number.
Folder name where AI Agent processing results will be saved. Default: aiagent_results
Name of the Container App used for zipping operations.
Port exposed by the zipping Container App.
Repository name in the ACR that contains the folder-zipper container image.
Tag for the folder-zipper container image.
Name for the Log Analytics workspace associated with the Container App.
Name for the Container Apps environment hosting the zipping application.
Name for the user-assigned managed identity used by the Container App to pull images.

Azure AD application (client) ID used for MSAL authentication. Default value is Zapper's pre-configured Entra ID. IMPORTANT: When using default credentials, email your deployed app URL to support@zapperedge.com for redirect URI configuration (completed within 12 hours). For more details, please check https://zapperedge.com/entra-configuration-guide

Authority URL used for MSAL authentication. Default uses Zapper's pre-configured Entra ID tenant. For custom Entra ID, provide your own authority URL and configure redirect URIs in your app registration.
Comma-separated list of known authorities for MSAL authentication. Default corresponds to Zapper's managed Entra ID tenant.
Optional domain hint used to streamline MSAL sign-in.
Client ID of a user-assigned managed identity with AcrPull rights on the ACR. Provide this when using a user-assigned identity for cross-subscription image pulls.
Resource ID of a user-assigned managed identity with AcrPull rights on the ACR. Provide this when using a user-assigned identity for cross-subscription image pulls.
Indicates whether to import a container image from another ACR. When true, supply source ACR details.
Name of the source Azure Container Registry when importing an image.
Resource group of the source ACR when importing an image.
Subscription ID of the source ACR when importing an image. Optional.
Repository name of the container image in the source ACR.
Tag of the container image in the source ACR.
Username for authenticating to the source ACR when it is private. Optional.
Password for authenticating to the source ACR when it is private. Optional.
Enable AI Agent feature for automated file processing via HTTP endpoint.
Repository name in the ACR that contains the AI Agent processor container image.
Tag for the AI Agent processor container image.
Enable Microsoft Sentinel integration for SIEM capabilities. When enabled, security events will be streamed to Log Analytics and Sentinel for threat detection and compliance monitoring.
Number of days to retain security logs in Log Analytics workspace. Valid range: 30-730 days. Longer retention increases storage costs.
Enable country-based geo-fencing for Partner Organizations. When enabled, an Azure Maps Gen2 account is created for IP geolocation services.
Name of the Azure Maps account used for geo-fencing IP geolocation. Derived from webAppName.

properties.scriptContent: state=$(az provider show --namespace Microsoft.KeyVault --query registrationState -o tsv || echo NotRegistered); if [ "$state" != "Registered" ]; then az provider register --namespace Microsoft.KeyVault; fi; echo "Microsoft.KeyVault RP state: $state"

properties.arguments: [format('--targetAcr {0} --targetImageName {1} --targetImageTag {2} --sourceAcr {3} --sourceImageName {4} --sourceImageTag {5} --sourceSubscription {6} --sourceResourceGroup {7} --sourceUsername {8} --sourcePassword {9} --targetSubscription {10}', parameters('acrName'), parameters('imageName'), parameters('imageTag'), parameters('sourceAcrName'), parameters('sourceImageName'), parameters('sourceImageTag'), parameters('sourceAcrSubscriptionId'), parameters('sourceAcrResourceGroup'), parameters('sourceAcrUsername'), parameters('sourceAcrPassword'), parameters('acrSubscriptionId'))]

properties.scriptContent:
set -euo pipefail
KV_ID="$(echo $1 | sed 's/^--kvId //')"
IS_RBAC=$(az resource show --ids "$KV_ID" --api-version 2023-07-01 --query "properties.enableRbacAuthorization" -o tsv 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$IS_RBAC" = "true" ]; then echo '{"isRbac":"true"}' > $AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH; else echo '{"isRbac":"false"}' > $AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH; fi

resources.comments: Legacy SecurityInsights solution - kept for backward compatibility
resources.name: [concat('SecurityInsights(', parameters('logAnalyticsWorkspaceName'), ')')]

resources.comments: Modern Sentinel onboarding state - ensures workspace is properly onboarded to Sentinel (API 2024-03-01)
resources.name: [concat(parameters('logAnalyticsWorkspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/default')]

resources.comments: Azure Maps Gen2 account for geo-fencing IP geolocation services
resources.name: [parameters('azureMapsAccountName')]
